brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +53.6% since Feb 27 — Brent $109.03 (Apr 2 close); WTI-Brent spread inverted; markets closed Apr 3 Good Friday; Apr 6 power plant deadline 3 days away
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 2 close: $109.03/bbl (+7.8% session). WTI-Brent spread inverted — unusual structure reflecting acute US domestic supply anxiety tied to Hormuz closure. March +~55% — largest monthly gain since inception in 1988. Markets closed Apr 3 (Good Friday). Goldman: $110 avg through March-April. Apr 6 energy strike deadline 3 days away. Iran-Oman transit protocol headlines drove intraday reversal. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +67.7% since Feb 27 — WTI $111.54 (Apr 2 close); first close above $100 since Jul 2022; WTI-Brent spread inverted; markets closed Apr 3 Good Friday
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 2 close: $111.54/bbl (+$11.42, +11.4%). First close above $100 since July 2022. WTI-Brent spread inverted — reflecting acute US domestic supply anxiety. Gas at $4.091/gal nationally (Apr 3 AAA). Diesel $5.43/gal. California ~$5.89/gal. Markets closed Apr 3 (Good Friday). Goldman: every $10/bbl adds ~$0.25/gal at pump. Apr 6 energy strike deadline 3 days away. Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.11 / +37.2% since Feb 27 — $4.091 nationally (Apr 3 AAA) — +$1.11 in 35 days — CA $5.89 — OK cheapest $3.27 — diesel $5.43 — largest monthly jump on record
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Apr 3: $4.091/gal nationally (AAA) — $1.11 surge in just 35 days. California leads at $5.89/gal; Oklahoma cheapest at $3.27/gal. Diesel averages $5.43/gal nationally, up 50% year-over-year. Monthly jump exceeds steepest climbs after Hurricane Katrina (2005) and Russia-Ukraine invasion (2022). Goldman: every $10/bbl crude increase adds ~$0.25/gal at pump. Source: AAA / EIA / Goldman Sachs.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -296 pts / -4.3% — S&P 6,582.69 (Apr 2 close, +0.11%); first weekly gain since conflict began (+3.4%); Dow 46,505; Nasdaq 21,879; markets closed Apr 3 Good Friday
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 2 close: 6,582.69 (+0.11%) — opened with sharp selloff post-Trump speech then staged dramatic intraday reversal on Iran-Oman transit protocol headlines. First weekly gain since conflict began (+3.4%). Dow 46,504.67 (-0.13%). Nasdaq 21,879.18 (+0.18%). VIX closed at ~23.87 (-2.73%). Markets closed Apr 3 (Good Friday). Gold $4,692.80 (-2.50%). 10Y Treasury 4.31%. LMT ~$622.89 (+30% YTD). RTX $193-197. XOM ~$160.69 (-5.2%). CVX ~$197.41 (-4.6%). DAL ~$58.78. UAL ~$91-95. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — Ford departs Split, resumes Eastern Med ops — Bush CSG transiting Atlantic ETA Apr 10-12 — 3 CSGs committed to theater — ~50,000 US troops — Apr 6 deadline 3 days
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 35: ~$1.0-1.5B/day. Ford departed Split, Croatia Apr 3 after repairs — resuming Eastern Med operations on Day ~284 of deployment. Bush CSG transiting Atlantic with CVW-7 — ETA CENTCOM ~Apr 10-12. Three carrier strike groups now committed to EUCOM/CENTCOM theater. 82nd Airborne 1st BCT operational in Kuwait. Total US forces ~50,000; up to 10,000 more planned. Navy: 292 ships total (233 USS, 59 USNS), 106 deployed, 86 underway. Apr 6 energy deadline 3 days. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / USNI / Reuters.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (35 Days)
total_cost.note: Pentagon $11.3B Day 6 + CSIS $16.5B Day 12 + ~$1-1.5B/day ongoing — ~$41.8B — Penn Wharton: $40-95B direct, up to $210B total economic impact — $200B supplemental faces bipartisan opposition — 71% oppose — 28% approval
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 35: ~$41.80B. Penn Wharton Budget Model: $40B-$95B in direct costs, up to $210B total economic impact. IranWarCost.com tracker incrementing ~$200M/day. $200B supplemental not yet formally sent to Congress; 71% oppose (CNN poll Apr 1). War approval collapsed to 28% (YouGov). IEA: 32 member states released 400M barrels from strategic reserves including 172M from US SPR. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / CNN.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 15,000+ targets struck — 3 CSGs committed to theater — Ford resumes ops after Split repairs — PAC-3 production tripling (7-yr Pentagon framework) — 850+ Tomahawks fired
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM/Pentagon: 15,000+ total targets struck. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~34,000+ through Day 35. 3 carrier strike groups now committed to EUCOM/CENTCOM theater. Ford departed Split Apr 3, resuming Eastern Med ops on Day ~284 of deployment. Pentagon announced 7-year framework to triple PAC-3 missile production. 850+ Tomahawk cruise missiles fired (most in any single campaign per CSIS). Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / CSIS.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13-15 US KIA — Trump speech: 13 KIA; CENTCOM: 15 including non-combat — 303-520+ WIA — The Intercept: Pentagon 'casualty cover-up' — ~750 troops wounded or killed since Oct 2023
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 13-15 KIA depending on source: Trump cited 13 in April 1 speech; CENTCOM reports 15 including non-combat deaths. Wounded: 303 per CENTCOM, potentially 520+ per The Intercept which reports Pentagon engaged in 'casualty cover-up.' ~750 troops wounded or killed across broader Middle East theater since October 2023. Key incidents: 6 soldiers killed at Camp Arifjan Kuwait (Mar 1); 6 airmen killed in KC-135 crash western Iraq (Mar 12). Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / The Intercept / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 303-520+ WIA (CENTCOM vs. The Intercept) — 315 RTD ~91% — 3 CSGs committed — Ford resumes ops — Tripoli operational — ~50,000 US troops in theater
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM: 303 WIA; The Intercept: potentially 520+. Pentagon Mar 31 briefing: 348 WIA (315 returned to duty ~91%; 6 remain seriously wounded). 3 carrier strike groups now committed to theater. Ford departed Split Apr 3 after repairs. Tripoli ARG/31st MEU (~3,500) operational. Boxer ARG en route (ETA mid-to-late April). Bush CSG transiting Atlantic (ETA ~Apr 10-12). Navy: 292 ships (233 USS, 59 USNS), 106 deployed, 86 underway. Total US forces ~50,000. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / USNI / Reuters.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 1,937+ killed, 24,800+ wounded — HRANA: 3,461+ (1,551 civilians, 236 children) — Iran Int'l: 4,700+ security forces — 83,351 buildings damaged — 3.2M displaced — IAEA cut off since Feb 26
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources reflecting fog of war and 30+ day internet blackout. Iranian Health Ministry: 1,937+ killed, 24,800+ wounded. HRANA independently documented 3,461+ deaths including 1,551 civilians. Iran International: 4,700+ security forces killed. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~8,000+. 83,351 residential buildings damaged. 65+ schools, 77+ healthcare facilities (31 major clinical, 12 inactive). 120+ historical/cultural heritage sites including UNESCO-listed Golestan Palace. 972 lbs of 60%-enriched uranium. IAEA communication severed since Feb 26. 3.2 million displaced. Internet at 4% of normal (NetBlocks). Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Iran International / OCHA / IAEA / NetBlocks.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,318+ killed (124 children) — 3,935+ wounded — 1.2M displaced (~25% of pop.) — 180,000+ crossed into Syria — IDF expanding to Litani — govt banned Hezbollah military activities
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli strikes and ground invasion since March 2. 1,318+ killed per Lebanese MoH (including 124 children). 3,935+ wounded. 1.2 million displaced (~25% of population), with 180,000+ crossing into Syria. Netanyahu ordered invasion expanded toward Litani River (Mar 29). DM Katz declared Israel will occupy southern Lebanon even after war. Lebanese govt banned Hezbollah military activities, expelled IRGC. PM Nawaf Salam condemned unauthorized attacks. Iran conditions ceasefire on ending Lebanon war. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / UNHCR / WHO.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4.4M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M — Lebanon 1.2M (~25% of pop.) — 180,000+ Lebanese crossed into Syria — ~2,000 vessels and ~20,000 seafarers stranded — Gulf consumer prices +40-120%
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. 4.4M+ total displaced. Iran: ~3.2 million internally displaced per UNHCR. Lebanon: ~1.2 million (~25% of population), with 180,000+ crossing into Syria. Israel: ~4,900 displaced. Iranian strikes on desalination plants in Kuwait and Qatar — which depend on them for 99% of drinking water — have created humanitarian crisis. Gulf consumer prices spiked 40-120%. ~2,000 vessels and ~20,000 seafarers stranded. OCHA activated contingency plans across 7 countries. Source: UNHCR / OCHA / WaPo / IMO.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — ~2,000 vessels stranded — ~20,000 seafarers — Hormuz ~6 transits/day — Iran-Oman transit protocol under negotiation — Philippine-flagged vessels allowed
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. ~2,000 vessels and ~20,000 seafarers stranded. Hormuz traffic down to ~6 transits/day from 130+ pre-conflict. Iran-Oman transit protocol under negotiation (Apr 2 — triggered market reversal). Iran agreed to allow Philippine-flagged vessels through following bilateral talks. IRGC 'tollbooth' at Larak Island charging up to $2M per crossing in yuan or crypto. Mar 1-25: only 142 total transits — down 93%. Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's / Bloomberg / Windward.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA: 236+ children (Iran) + Lebanon 124+ + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — IRGC cluster bombs wounding children in Bnei Brak — 600+ schools attacked — Minab school 165+ killed
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 236+ children killed in Iran. Lebanon: 124+ children (updated Lebanese MoH). Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. IRGC cluster-warhead missiles continue targeting Israeli cities — children critically wounded in Bnei Brak. Minab school (Feb 28): 165-170 killed, mostly girls aged 7-12. 600+ educational centers attacked in Iran per Red Crescent. 65+ schools destroyed. Bushehr NPP struck 3 times — nearest impact 350m from reactor. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / UNHRC / ICRC.
